tooling.screenshots: Playwright 1.59.1 / Chromium headless. Viewport 1280×800 (desktop), 375×812 (mobile). networkidle wait + 800ms settle.
tooling.comp: Inner Pages.html captured fullPage then cropped per .page-divider anchor via sharp 0.34.5.
tooling.lighthouse: Lighthouse 13.2.0 / form-factor=mobile / throttling=simulate / Chromium headless. Single run per route. Re-ran failed routes once.
tooling.linkCrawl: Python urllib over the 7 routes; HEAD-style GET for every internal href found across all pages.
tooling.schema: JSON-LD blocks read directly from emitted HTML. Run https://search.google.com/test/rich-results manually for the official validator pass.

visualFidelity._note: Each route lists what's faithful to the comp and what diverged. Comp source for inner pages is the corresponding section in Inner Pages.html.

notes.text: Topbar / sticky white nav / hero photo + green-and-red text-shadow stack on the wordmark / Tonight strip aside / marquee / squiggle dividers all lift faithfully.
Week grid renders today's date highlight client-side (Friday at capture time).

Comp's static "Tonight at Terry's" is a single info card with 4 manual rows; build renders the CMS_SCHEMA tonightStrip[] (4 cells of tag/title/meta). Different shape, similar visual weight. Per SLOT_RECONCILIATION.

Hero background photo: comp uses photo-room-hero.jpg as a wide interior; the file at that name in the handoff was actually a server-with-poboy + dining-room-background shot (post-rename it stays at photo-room-hero.jpg). The hero with overlay still works as a sense-of-room shot; flag if Design wants a true wide interior.
Heritage stat row: comp shows 45 / 3 / 7-day; build mirrors. Confirmed in /about hero for full picture.
Hero, anchor nav, two-column section layout, dot-leader rows with prices all lifted faithfully.
Daily Lunch aside (price flag + 5-day list) renders from menu.lunchSpecial.

Comp shows ~30 items hand-curated across 4 sections; build lifts ~50 items across 8 sections from the actual PDF (Sandwiches, Subs, Dinners added — the comp omitted them). Result: longer page, more complete coverage; visually the same component pattern.
Wing/sub tier pricing (10/20/30/50, Sm/Lg) renders inline as a single line; comp had multi-row entries for wings. Single-line is denser; flag if Design wants stacked rows.
Drinks card + happy-hour callout + cash-discount footnote + consumer notice all rendered.
Hero with right-rail group photo, two flipped story rows with green pull quote, family row of three cards.
Family-card portraits render <DraftField> red-dashed pending boxes per CONTENT-INVENTORY 🔴.

Family names retracted per Design pre-walk ruling (2026-05-01): the agency-drafted placeholders 'Terry Sr.', 'Jr.', 'III (Tre)' were never confirmed and inventing names for a real family business reads worse than blank. All three slots now render as DraftField pending pills ([OWNER NAME PENDING] / [2ND GEN NAME PENDING] / [3RD GEN NAME PENDING]) and Eli's checklist has three explicit name asks (§3 below).

Story-row bodies on /about were rewritten to remove fabricated named references — the founder's narrative now ends with a [pending] callout in place of named anecdote, and the second/third-generation row similarly defers names. Factual claims (1980 opening, 11 sauces, 22 TVs, hand-breaded wings, etc.) preserved as written; whether those need confirmation too is a §3 question.
Pull-quote text matches comp verbatim: "If a guy can't eat it all, you cooked it right." (Quote isn't tied to a fabricated name — kept.)
6-card grid (5 weekday + Sunday Football hot card) lifted faithfully. Per-day descriptions match comp.

Comp has descriptions inline in HTML. Build keeps them inline in the page.tsx via WEEKDAY_DESC map (not in content JSON). Future client-edit means a code change. Worth promoting to specials.json if Eli wants to own those descriptions.
Happy-hour mention with placeholder narrative below the grid; pour list flagged as pending.
"22 TVs · sound on the Gators" tagline, On-the-Screens copy, two CTAs (call to reserve + private game-day bookings).

Comp shows a sample TV list with 5 specific games (Florida vs Tennessee, Alabama vs South Carolina, etc.). Build renders an empty-state card ("Lineup posted weekly during football season") because content/sports.json:lineup[] is empty. v1 is correct per brief out-of-scope decision; flag for client to populate weekly.
Build adds a "Pick the Room" 4-card section (Bar / Dining / Billiards / Patio) — implicit in the brief but not drawn in the comp. Editorial drift; rule from Design wanted.
Map placeholder replaced with a real Google Maps iframe (no API key, q= search). Address + phone + hours table all rendered.
Hours table highlights today via a tiny inline script (`tr.today` class).
Comp's hours-table placeholder used Tuesday as today; build computes actual today (NY tz). Not a fidelity break — same component, real data.
Build adds two CTAs below the map (Get Directions / Open in Maps). Comp had nothing under the map. Editorial add — rule from Design wanted.
Two-column layout, drop-a-line copy, hand-script accent line, contact form panel.
Form fields differ slightly: comp had First Name / Last Name split; build has single Name. Cleaner UX, same data shape (all flows into Static Forms anyway). Flag if Design wants the split.

Topic dropdown options match CMS_SCHEMA forms.contact.topics: General / Catering / Private Event / Press. Comp's options were Private party / Catering / Feedback / General — superset overlapping ~70%. Used CMS_SCHEMA values as canonical.
Pending-recipient DraftField surfaces below the copy when env recipient is the placeholder. Visible in the live preview (Eli should see a red pill labeled "PENDING: Real contact recipient email").

photoAudit._note: All photos in public/assets/, with judgment + best-current-use. Filename-vs-content fix happened mid-build (see editorial drift). All current names match contents.

inventory.notes: Server with po-boy in foreground; dining-room booths visible in background. With navy overlay it reads as 'inside Terry's' but isn't a true wide interior. Replace with a clean wide-shot if a shoot happens.

inventory.use: About hero right rail · Home heritage section
inventory.notes: 8 staff in front of brushed-metal Terry's wordmark. Real, on-brand, and the closest thing we have to the three-generations photo. Keep, but commission a real owner+family portrait alongside it.

inventory.use: About story-row 2 · Home Belly Buster card
inventory.notes: Server holding burger and wings basket in front of the Terry's metal sign. Clean, on-brand. Vertical orientation works for both portrait crops.

inventory.use: Home Original Wings card · /menu wings hero (when added)
inventory.notes: Wings basket with three cocktails on the bar. Bar-program shot, doubles as wings hero. The one drink shot we have.
Steak with mushrooms and onion rings — close-crop, low-light. Reads as a steak photo but the lighting and angle are diner-style; would benefit from professional food photography.
Chicken sandwich + fries in basket. Same diner-photography style. Wide aspect (1500×650) limits use as a square card thumbnail.
Pool tables and air hockey, dim warm light, includes the Terry's hat shelf in background. Genuine room atmosphere.
Promotional graphic (text overlay + product shot), not a candid photo. Reads as marketing collateral, not editorial. A real game-day room photo would be a step-change improvement.
Two camo Terry's hats with leather patch. Sharp, distinct, branded. Best of the merch shots.
Hats on shelves, low light, slightly soft focus. Works as supporting imagery but not as a hero.
Raster of menu PDF page 1. Used to seed menu.json; should NOT be embedded on the live site.

inventory.use: /visit hero banner above the map+address grid
inventory.notes: Mural-side of the building — yellow stucco with the hand-painted Terry's wordmark in green/red graffiti style (matches the site's color palette exactly), red tropical cocktail with '5 5 5' clock face, palm trees, 'It's 5 o'clock here!' in teal. Real Florida, real Terry's. Daytime shot; wishlist still reserves a golden-hour upgrade for v1.5.

halfDayShootWishlist.subject: Owner / family portrait — three generations, named
halfDayShootWishlist.why: Biggest content gap. The three-generations narrative on /about has no faces attached, currently leaning on the staff-group photo as a stand-in. A staged owner portrait (or three small portraits) would fill the family[] slots and unblock the launch.
halfDayShootWishlist.ref: /about § Three Terrys family row (currently rendering DraftField boxes)

halfDayShootWishlist.subject: Sunday game-day room shot — packed crowd watching a Gators or NFL game
halfDayShootWishlist.why: The current /specials Sunday card uses a promo graphic where it should show atmosphere. A wide candid of the bar room mid-game replaces marketing with proof.

halfDayShootWishlist.subject: Exterior storefront at golden hour (upgrade)
halfDayShootWishlist.why: Mural-side daylight shot (photo-exterior.jpg) shipped 2026-05-01 — fills the /visit gap as a hero banner above the map. Doesn't replace the wishlist item entirely; a golden-hour shot would still be more atmospheric and reusable for Google Business Profile + Visit page seasonal swaps.

halfDayShootWishlist.subject: Wide dining-room interior at service time
halfDayShootWishlist.why: Replaces the current Home hero background (a foreground food shot) with a true room-feel wide. The comp intent for the hero was a wide interior; we don't have one.

halfDayShootWishlist.subject: Cocktail / bar program flight (3–5 drinks, behind-the-bar lighting)
halfDayShootWishlist.why: The bar program differentiator on the existing site is invisible. One bar-craft shot validates the "fully stocked liquor selection" copy.
halfDayShootWishlist.ref: /menu § Drinks & Bar · About story row 2 · future Bar callout

halfDayShootWishlist.subject: Trivia night candid (Tuesday) and karaoke night candid (Friday)
halfDayShootWishlist.why: Events grid promotes both weekly; build has no atmosphere photo for either. One photo per program would carry these cards visually for weeks.

halfDayShootWishlist.subject: Half-pound burger overhead, single-flavor wings overhead — pro food photography
halfDayShootWishlist.why: Current food photos are diner-quality candids. For the home food strip and menu hero, replacement-grade hero food shots would lift the whole brand register.
halfDayShootWishlist.ref: Home § What You Came For · /menu hero

photoAudit.filenameContentNote: Mid-build the design-handoff package shipped 7 photos with names that didn't match contents (e.g., photo-staff-group.jpg actually contained the Sunday Football promo graphic; photo-staff-burger-wings.jpg was an AI-generated illustration). Code renamed each file to match its actual content; the AI-illustration was dropped (no real-photo slot fits it). Going forward: every Facebook pull will need the same audit before it goes into the bundle. Building a check into the next photo-fetch script is recommended.

contentStatus._note: Single table of every blocked / placeholder / analogous-fill content item. The point: Eli's review is a checklist, not a scavenger hunt.

items.source: 🔴 retracted — Design ruled the agency-drafted placeholder out 2026-05-01
items.question: Founder's full name (first + last). Goes in the family-card name slot in big display type.
Second-generation operator's name (the kitchen lead). Goes in the family-card name slot.
Third-generation operator's name (front of house). Goes in the family-card name slot.
Three portraits — photographed individually or staged together. Half-day shoot wishlist #1.

items.source: 🔴 retracted with names; founder's 1980 kept (live-site fact)
items.question: Confirm the year each second- and third-generation operator took over. Founder's 1980 = the year doors opened, kept as established fact.

items.currentValue: Retracted: agency-drafted named anecdote replaced with a [pending] line in the storyRow body. Factual claims (1980, 11 sauces, 22 TVs) preserved.
items.question: Confirm or supply the real founder + generations narrative. 30-min phone call w/ owner. Also: confirm the preserved factual claims (11 sauces, 22 TVs, etc.) are accurate as printed.
Real recipient email for catering / private events / press / general.
Real Facebook page URL, OR confirm "no Facebook" decision (icon stays hidden).
Any other socials worth linking? Post-launch chase-able.
Confirm Saturday hours — closed, or 11am–12am as Design assumed?
Real kitchen-close offset (e.g., 30min before bar close, or different per day)?
Real trivia host name + frequency + cover policy.

items.field: Friday Karaoke / Sat Live Music host
items.currentValue: no host name (Karaoke Night) / "Acoustic Live Music"
items.question: Real karaoke host, real live-music host(s), real frequency.
Drink specials, food specials, half-priced apps — actual list per CONTENT-INVENTORY.

items.source: v2 manual / API decision per brief out-of-scope
items.question: v1: do you want to manually populate weekly, or leave empty until v2 sports-API integration?

items.currentValue: Agency-drafted entries (Best Wings / Ladies' Night / Pool Hall)
items.question: Replace at v1 with real entries, or carry the agency drafts and migrate WP posts at v2?
Confirm the 45 (years) is correct as of 2026 — comp said 45. If launch slips into 2026 and we're now at 46, update.
Confirm exact coordinates for Restaurant schema and Google Maps deep-link. Easy to verify against the actual address.
Original AI/EPS source — or confirm we ship retina PNGs only. Affects scaling at very large sizes (e.g. printable promos).

lighthouse._note: Mobile form-factor, simulated throttling, single run per route. Targets per brief: Perf ≥90 / A11y ≥95 / Best Practices ≥95 / SEO 100. Re-run against the consolidated fix pass deploy (commit e6c14d9).

results.verdict: PARTIAL — Perf 75 (LCP 6.4s persists despite Next/Image priority + preload; LCP element identification didn't return on Lighthouse rate-limited run; queue for v1.1 deeper-dive)
PARTIAL — Perf 88 (target 90, missed by 2). LCP 3.4s acceptable on mobile. Likely menu-section hydration; v1.1 polish.

lighthouse.summary: Consolidated fix pass landed strong A11y wins: every route now ≥95 (was 92-97 with two routes failing). /menu lifted from 92 → 100 (heading-order fix + main landmark + h2 promotion). /about lifted to 100. Three routes at 98, perfect on the inner pages. Color-contrast fixes (--red → --red-soft on .t-eyebrow--red and .kind.is-hot; --red → --red-2 on menu badges) shipped clean. Performance: home and /menu remain below 90 target. Home LCP 6.4s persists despite the Next/Image priority + automatic preload — Lighthouse's LCP-element audit didn't return on this rate-limited run, so we don't yet know what's driving it. /menu at 88 is one renderer-pass shy of 90. Both routes flagged for v1.1 deep-dive; the brief's target is ≥90 and 5 of 7 routes meet it cleanly.
wins: A11y went from 92-97 (two failing routes) to 96-100 (all passing)
Every route emits a <main> landmark (was missing on all 7 site routes)
/menu heading sequence now h1 → h2 → h2 → h3 (Footer); was h1 → h3 → h4 with skips
All 7 routes emit canonical URLs (was zero)
/specials /sports /visit /contact each have one h1 (was zero — used h2 for page title)
remaining_v1_1: Home Lighthouse Perf 75: hero photo is now a Next/Image priority but LCP didn't budge from 6.4s. Element-attribution didn't return on the rate-limited Lighthouse runs. Need a hands-on profiler session.
/menu Lighthouse Perf 88: 2 points shy of 90. Likely the long menu-section hydration path; could benefit from streaming or lazy-loading below-fold sections.

emitted.validates: Run https://search.google.com/test/rich-results manually with the URL — automated validation not run
emitted.note: Includes OpeningHoursSpecification (×7), GeoCoordinates, PostalAddress, hasMenu, foundingDate, paymentAccepted.

emitted.validates: Run https://search.google.com/test/rich-results manually with the URL — automated validation not run
emitted.note: Includes 8 MenuSection blocks; each MenuItem has Offer with USD price (or array of Offers for tiered pricing).

missing.rationale: Trivia / karaoke / live-music / Sunday football could be modeled as Event schema. Helps AI citations on "trivia night Ocala" / "karaoke Friday near me" queries. Out of v1 scope but cheap to add at v1.1.
Q&A like "Do you take reservations?" / "Is there a kids' menu?" / "Do you have parking?" — strong candidate for SGE / AI-citation surfaces.
Once Google reviews are wired (post Google Business Profile claim), the Restaurant schema can include aggregateRating to surface star counts in SERPs.
Inner routes don't emit BreadcrumbList. Marginal value; ship in a polish pass.

consoleErrors._note: Captured during desktop-viewport playwright run. 0 errors / 0 warnings unless noted.

perRoute.details: One Google Maps iframe console warning (third-party). Non-fatal.

linkCrawl._note: All internal hrefs across the 7 routes hit live; non-200 codes flagged here.
linkCrawl.verdict: PASS — all 7 site routes return 200. Static asset paths (/_next/...) and the menu PDF (/assets/menu-2025.pdf) all 200. tel: links not HTTP-checked.

contactFormTest.payload: [AUDIT TEST] message body, name=AUDIT TEST, replyTo=audit-test@grm-internal.test, recipient=pending@terrys.place
contactFormTest.verdict: PASS — Static Forms accepted the submission. Eli must confirm the recipient placeholder mailbox (`pending@terrys.place`) is being monitored OR replace it with a real address before client review.

perRoute.title: Terry's Place · Ocala's Sports Bar & Grill Since 1980
perRoute.description: Family-run sports bar and grill in Ocala, FL since 1980. Half-pound burgers, hand-cut steaks, daily-baked bread. Wall-to-wall TVs, NFL Sunday Ticket, three generations.

perRoute.title: Menu · Terry's Place
perRoute.description: Hand-cut steaks, half-pound burgers, jumbo wings, daily-baked subs. Daily $10.50 lunch Mon–Fri.

perRoute.title: Three Generations · Terry's Place
perRoute.description: Family-run since 1980. Three generations behind the bar in Ocala, FL.

perRoute.title: Specials & Lunch · Terry's Place
perRoute.description: $10.50 daily lunch Mon–Fri. Sunday Football wings + pitcher special. Happy Hour weekday afternoons.

perRoute.title: 22 TVs · Sports · Terry's Place
perRoute.description: NFL Sunday Ticket, Florida Gators, college football. 22 wall-to-wall screens across four rooms.

perRoute.title: Visit · 4121 NE 36th Ave, Ocala FL · Terry's Place
perRoute.description: 4121 NE 36th Ave, Ocala FL 34479. Open seven days. Free parking. (352) 732-3820.

perRoute.title: Contact · Terry's Place
perRoute.description: Catering, private events, press, or just a question — we'll get back to you.

fixesNeeded: ✅ FIXED 2026-05-01 (commit c8bc76f): every route emits a self-canonical URL via metadata.alternates.canonical.
✅ FIXED 2026-05-01 (commit c8bc76f): /specials /sports /visit /contact each have one h1 (the inner-display heading) carrying the page title.

aiCitationPosture.thesis: GRM's ranking-from-day-one thesis works for contractor sites (Page 1 = thin Yellow-Pages aggregators) but applies only partially to restaurants. AI-citation surfaces for restaurants are dominated by Yelp, Google Business Profile, TripAdvisor, OpenTable. terrys.place will not displace those — but it can be a strong supporting citation, especially for hyperlocal niche queries.

winnableQueries.rationale: Local intent + qualifier the GBP listing alone won't dominate. Restaurant schema + Menu schema + an emitted Event schema for game days = strong SGE/AI-citation candidate.

winnableQueries.q: where to watch the Gators in Ocala
winnableQueries.rationale: Niche, sport-specific, conversational query. The 'sound on the Gators · 22 TVs · NFL Sunday Ticket' content directly answers it. Yelp + GBP weakly answer this; the site can win on specificity.

winnableQueries.q: Ocala restaurant with karaoke / trivia night
winnableQueries.rationale: Event-specific niche. v1.1 Event schema for trivia (Tuesday) and karaoke (Friday) materially improves citation odds.

winnableQueries.q: $10 lunch Ocala / cheap lunch near me Marion County
winnableQueries.rationale: Daily Lunch program at $10.50 is unusually concrete. Page is already structured around this value-prop.
If site adds a Kids/Family copy section + FAQ schema ("Do you have a kids' menu?" "Is the patio family-friendly?"), this becomes winnable. Currently no Kids content.

needsClientWork: Google Business Profile — claim, update hours, post weekly events, add the new menu PDF. This is the single highest-leverage hour Eli can spend post-launch.
Yelp listing — claim, update hours, replace photos with the new shoot's output.
TripAdvisor — optional but free. Most travelers checking 'sports bar Ocala' see TA results before SERP.
Apple Maps / Bing Places — quick wins, often unclaimed.
Google Reviews — actively solicit during the launch window. AggregateRating in Restaurant schema requires real reviews.
v1_1_recommendations: Add Event schema for the recurring weekly programs (trivia, karaoke, Sunday football) — generated from events.weekly.
Add FAQPage schema on /visit (parking, kids, dress code, age policy at events) and /contact (private events, catering, large parties).
Add LocalBusiness reviews aggregateRating to Restaurant schema once Google Reviews count crosses ~10 ratings.
Self-host a /reviews route that pulls a Google Reviews widget (or static cache of top 5) — gives crawlers something to index that backs the aggregateRating.
aiCitationPosture.wpSiteDiff: The existing WordPress terrys.place currently ranks for hyperlocal queries ("Terry's Place Ocala", "Terry's Sports Bar Ocala"). At cutover, replacing the WP install with the new build means: (a) the existing page slugs change (e.g., /our-establishment vs /sports — the latter is canonical in the new IA but loses the legacy URL's authority); (b) any backlinks pointing at /our-menu, /about-us, /contact-terrys-sports-bar-and-grill etc. need 301 redirects in vercel.json or via rewrites in next.config.ts. Recommendation: implement a redirect map at cutover for the 7 legacy WP URLs to their new equivalents.

editorialDrift.what: menu.items[].price relaxed from integer to number
editorialDrift.compSpec: CMS_SCHEMA originally specified `"type": "integer", "description": "USD, no $. Whole dollars only."`
editorialDrift.shipped: Schema relaxed to `number` (decimal). All prices in menu.json are decimals (e.g. 9.29, 14.99).
editorialDrift.why: The actual menu PDF has cents on every item ($10.29, $9.29, $11.99). Integer-only would have required dropping the cents column or rounding everything. SLOTS.md's example item also showed `9.29`. Treated this as a transcription error in the schema rather than design intent.

editorialDrift.compSpec: Not specified in CMS_SCHEMA; SLOTS.md showed a single `price` per item.
editorialDrift.shipped: Added optional `pricing: [{size, price}]` array for sized/tiered items (wings 10/20/30/50, subs Sm/Lg, fish dinner 2-piece/3-piece). MenuRow component renders these as a single line.
editorialDrift.why: Several real menu items have tiered pricing that doesn't fit a single number. Reverse-compatible with existing single-price items.

editorialDrift.what: tonightStrip uses the CMS_SCHEMA shape, not the SLOTS.md heroCard shape
editorialDrift.compSpec: SLOTS.md described an old `heroCard` shape with label / leadLine / supportLine / rows[]. CMS_SCHEMA defined `tonightStrip` as 4 cells of {tag, title, meta}.
editorialDrift.shipped: tonightStrip with 4 cells per CMS_SCHEMA. Home renders these as a vertical strip in the hero aside.
editorialDrift.why: SLOT_RECONCILIATION.md was explicit: `heroCard → tonightStrip` and CMS_SCHEMA wins for naming. The visual treatment is similar (right-rail dark card with content), just modeled as a uniform array.

editorialDrift.shipped: Added `storyRows[]` with `{kicker, title, body[], image, imageAlt, pullQuote, flip}` matching the comp's two-row beginning/today layout.
editorialDrift.why: The Inner Pages /about comp clearly intended structured story rows. Without this in schema, the body would have been free-text markdown — losing the per-row image + pull-quote affordances. Adding the slot was cheaper than abandoning the comp's structure.

editorialDrift.what: Saturday hours filled in (live site missing)
editorialDrift.compSpec: Live site terrys.place doesn't show Saturday at all; CMS_SCHEMA default is null.
editorialDrift.shipped: hours.6 = {open: 11:00, close: 24:00}, matching the comp footer and SLOTS.md.
editorialDrift.why: Sports bar with empty Saturday is implausible. Comp + SLOTS both implied Saturday is open. Flagged as a 🟡 placeholder for client confirmation. If client confirms closed, swap to null.

editorialDrift.compSpec: CMS_SCHEMA original had: site / hours / tonightStrip / menu / about / events. SLOT_RECONCILIATION referenced: + specials / sports / news / forms / seo / theme / legal.
editorialDrift.shipped: Added all 7 namespaces with shapes inferred from Inner Pages comp + SLOTS.md mappings.
editorialDrift.why: Schema-completion was an agreed-upon Code task (option (a) from intake). Original CMS_SCHEMA was incomplete relative to SLOT_RECONCILIATION; completion was a prereq for content-driven rendering of /specials, /sports, /news, /contact.

editorialDrift.compSpec: Per IMAGE-USAGE the file should have been 'Server holding loaded burger + wings basket'.
editorialDrift.shipped: Renamed the real server-with-burger-wings file (which was at photo-sunday-football.jpg) to photo-staff-burger-wings.jpg. The AI-illustrated bar scene that was sitting at this name is dropped from the bundle.
editorialDrift.why: AI-generated image doesn't fit the brand register and shouldn't ship. No real-photo slot exists for it.

editorialDrift.what: Facebook icon hidden when site.social.facebook is null
editorialDrift.compSpec: Comp showed an `f` literal — a placeholder link.
editorialDrift.shipped: Header conditionally renders the icon: `{site.social.facebook && <a ...>}`. Empty = no icon.
editorialDrift.why: Per the brief: "hide the social icon if empty rather than DraftField — cleaner." Live site's broken `href="#"` was the bug we're avoiding repeating.

editorialDrift.compSpec: Brief: news.recent in content/news.json, 3 entries, no detail pages in v1.
editorialDrift.shipped: content/news.json with 3 agency-drafted entries; cards have `url: null` (no detail link).
editorialDrift.why: Settled at brief intake (option (a)). Reduces v1 surface area; can light up at v2 when client writes real posts or migrates WP.

editorialDrift.what: /sports adds a 'Pick the Room' 4-card section not in the comp
editorialDrift.compSpec: Inner Pages /sports comp had hero copy + TV list. No rooms grid.
editorialDrift.shipped: Build adds a 4-card 'Pick the Room' grid (Bar / Dining / Billiards / Patio) below the TV list.
editorialDrift.why: Establishment copy on the live site emphasizes the four rooms. Promoting them to /sports felt aligned. Editorial add — Design rules on whether to keep.

editorialDrift.what: /visit adds 'Get Directions / Open in Maps' CTAs below the embedded map
editorialDrift.compSpec: Inner Pages /visit comp had only the map placeholder.
editorialDrift.shipped: Build adds two t-btn buttons below the iframe.
editorialDrift.why: Mobile users routinely want to launch the directions in Maps app, not zoom an embedded iframe. UX add — Design rules on whether to keep.

editorialDrift.what: Contact form: single Name field instead of First/Last split
editorialDrift.compSpec: Inner Pages /contact comp had First Name + Last Name as separate fields.
editorialDrift.why: Cleaner form on mobile, fewer fields to fill. Static Forms doesn't care about the split. Design rules on whether to revise.

editorialDrift.what: /specials weekday descriptions inline in page.tsx (not in JSON)
editorialDrift.compSpec: Comp had per-day descriptions inline in HTML; specials.json doesn't model them.
editorialDrift.shipped: WEEKDAY_DESC dict in src/app/specials/page.tsx — five strings hard-coded.
editorialDrift.why: Schema doesn't currently carry per-day desc, and the strings are agency copy not strictly client-editable. Promote to specials.weekday.days[].desc if Eli wants to own these.
